_README: Interpretive text for the public leaderboard page. THESE ARE CLAIMS ABOUT RESULTS — they are only true of the runs named below. export_site.py hard-fails if for_dataset_version / for_config_hash do not match the sweep being exported, and cross-checks band_separated + floor_note against the actual data. If a re-run changes the picture, rewrite this file; do not loosen the checks.

headline_findings.label: No search, no answers
headline_findings.detail: With tools off it answers none of them. Every point here is earned by searching.

headline_findings.label: Easy vs hard retrieval
headline_findings.detail: Pooled over nine on-device models. The cloud references do not drop at all.

headline_findings.label: The cost of thinking
headline_findings.detail: Reasoning mode: 2–6x the tokens for +0.02 correctness (p=0.64). We run it off.

config_notes.provider: Brave. The one configuration choice that clearly matters: 0.74 vs 0.59 against Tavily (p=0.004). Everything else in this table is worth less than it looks.
config_notes.result_format: Markdown. Also cannot be proven alone (+0.02, p=0.63). Like the tool descriptions, it earns its place only as part of the bundle.
config_notes.tool_desc: Enriched: tell the model what the tool is for and to write short keyword queries. Cannot be proven alone (+0.03, p=0.48) — but it is part of a bundle that doubles quality, so do not cherry-pick it out.
config_notes.result_count: Five results is the shipped default and nothing beat it. Note that asking for more can be a no-op: the result menu is capped by a token ceiling that silently drops trailing hits.
config_notes.snippet_chars: 280-character snippets retained. Shorter truncation lost information; full-length snippets cost many more tokens for no gain.
config_notes.read_url_policy: Page reading stays available but earns its keep rarely — snippets answer most questions, and models call read_url on well under half a page per question.
config_notes.max_turns: A five-turn budget is not the binding constraint: the winning configurations average under two turns per question.
config_notes.system_prompt: Kept, but it earns nothing. Stripping every instruction from it — leaving only 'You are a helpful assistant' — scored identically (p=0.90). The tool description, not the system prompt, is what makes a model search.
config_notes.enable_thinking: Off. Reasoning mode costs 2-6x the generated tokens for no measurable gain in answer quality — a bad trade on a phone, in a loop already full of search results. Turning it off also makes every model comparable.

metric_definitions.label: Fresh ✓
metric_definitions.description: Share of retrieval-required questions answered correctly. These ask about events after the models' training data, so they cannot be answered from memory. This is the primary score.

metric_definitions.label: Stable ✓
metric_definitions.description: Share of timeless general-knowledge questions answered correctly. Checks that adding web search does not damage what a model already knew.

metric_definitions.label: Searched
metric_definitions.description: How often the model actually searches on a question that requires it. The dominant failure mode for small models is not searching at all.

metric_definitions.label: False searches
metric_definitions.description: How often the model searches on requests that need no web access, such as writing a haiku or doing arithmetic. Wasted latency and API calls.

metric_definitions.label: Valid tool calls
metric_definitions.description: Share of the model's tool calls that are well-formed and executable. A model that cannot produce valid calls cannot search at all.

metric_definitions.label: Turns
metric_definitions.description: Average round trips through the search loop per question. On a phone each turn is a full prompt re-processing, so turns dominate response time.

metric_definitions.label: Prompt tokens
metric_definitions.description: Average tokens sent to the model per question, summed across the loop. Search results and fetched pages dominate it. On a phone this is the number that sets how long an answer takes and how much context is left for the answer itself.

gate_failures.title: The tool definitions never reach these models
gate_failures.mechanism: All five ship chat templates that never render the tool definitions. The runtime decides whether a model supports tool calling by test-rendering its template; if the template ignores tools, the schemas are silently left out of the prompt. Measured: these five receive 105-146 tokens (system line + question); every working model receives 363-576. The missing tokens are the tool definitions. This is not a verdict on the models — they were never offered the tools. Gemma-3 fails visibly, improvising Google's tool_code syntax. Hermes-3 fails dangerously, inventing fake citations. Phi-4-mini says it cannot browse — given what it was sent, that is honest. The fix is a tool-declaring chat template.

limitations: Questions are single-fact and English-only, drawn from one eight-week news window. A model tuned on other topics or languages could rank differently.
Correctness is graded by an LLM judge against a human-verified gold answer. The judge was validated against hand-labelled samples, but it is not a human grader.
The configuration was tuned on an earlier question set and then frozen. These scores all use that same frozen setup; they are not each model's best possible score.
Models are compared at the quantisation shown in the table. Three run at a higher or lower bit-width than the rest, which is a real difference in the weights, not just the wrapper.
Quality is measured on a workstation running the same quantised model files a phone would run. Battery, heat and on-device latency are not measured; turns and prompt size are the stand-ins.
Fresh questions go stale by design. Scores are only comparable within one dataset version, which is why the version is stamped on this page.
Each question is asked once, so differences of a few points between neighbouring models are not resolved.
band_note: This board does resolve an order. Harder questions — ones needing several sources, or a second search that depends on the first — separate the models that everyday lookups could not: the top on-device model is now clearly ahead of the bottom of the working band. Neighbouring rows still sit within noise of each other, so read the groupings rather than the exact positions.
config_lift_note: The whole bundle — enriched tool descriptions, Brave, markdown results — is worth 0.74 vs 0.36 against the app's original settings (p under 0.0001): it roughly doubles answer quality. But almost none of that survives being taken apart. Only the search provider is individually significant. The changes compound; ship them together or not at all.

tier_notes.T1: “Who won the final?” The answer is right there in the search results.
tier_notes.T2: The answer is inside the article, not in the search snippet, so the model has to open the page.
tier_notes.T3: Needs two different sources put together; no single page says it.
tier_notes.T4: Needs a second search that depends on what the first one found.

frontier_note: The two reference models are cloud systems, shown for scale only and kept out of the ranking. The best on-device model lands close enough to them that this dataset cannot resolve the gap — which is not the same as matching them: its losses are concentrated in the hardest questions.
variants_note: We also tested an abliterated community variant of Qwen3.5-2B against the official checkpoint under identical conditions. They scored exactly the same (38 of 53 each). Abliteration buys nothing here, so we rank official checkpoints only.
gate_failure_takeaway: Gate on rendered capability, never a model allowlist. Pass tools, then check the rendered prompt: under ~300 tokens means the schemas never landed. One assertion catches all five, and any future repack that drops the tool template.
